finding_title: Reward debt is updated after balance mutation, enabling excess reward claims
summary: When a user withdraws before reward debt is synchronized, the protocol can calculate pending rewards from an outdated baseline and allow the user to receive more rewards than fairly accrued.
root_cause: state desynchronization in reward accounting after balance mutation
broken_invariants: user rewards must remain proportional to effective stake over time
reward debt must stay aligned with user balance checkpoints
attack_preconditions: the protocol uses cumulative reward accounting
withdraw mutates user balance before reward debt sync
claim path pays out based on stale accounting state

impact.description: an attacker can claim more reward tokens than intended by the accounting model

why_not_higher: the issue leaks reward value but does not directly compromise principal assets in this simplified scenario
why_not_lower: the exploit path is permissionless, repeatable, and can create measurable reward overpayment
common_false_positive_angles: the protocol may intentionally defer settlement and compensate later in the same execution path
claim path may force a safe synchronization before transfer
mitigations: synchronize reward debt before mutating user balance
assert reward baseline consistency before payout

snippets.code:
user.amount = user.amount - withdrawAmount;
user.rewardDebt = accRewardPerShare * user.amount / PRECISION;
snippets.note: balance is mutated before reward baseline is safely settled
